title: cotoneaster integerrimus var. erythrocarpus
intro: Cotoneaster integerrimus var. erythrocarpus, also known as the red-fruited cotoneaster, is a deciduous shrub native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and improve digestion. In herbal preparations, the red berries and leaves of the Cotoneaster integerrimus var. erythrocarpus are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
